generatedSentence: It was downright nutty how Molly, a husky newcomer, decided to meet abroad. Her initial voyages to distant junkyards, though often overrated, continued, keeping sentimental keepsakes from each adventure. She wore her lucky underwear, an exaggerated detail, while the binding on her partner’s banjoes seemed to mystify others. This new voyage promised overviews far from any shock. The oracle’s ominous warning brought panic; an iced rainfall, thick with strange pollen, descended upon their ancestral vineyard. Despite the iffy backstory and a fifty-year-old circuit omen, Molly, unfazed, showed no hesitation. Others, not so brave, became crybabies. She knew this impersonal hazard, flanked by dark clouds, would test their kinder spirit, leaving their knickers soaked. The goldmine of knowledge from an untitled inkwell revealed an omniscient pyramid, its oxide glowing. Molly, jacketed and resolute, understood the full backstory of the witchcraft and the rising conflict. She knew the jury was out, but her masterful plan, divvied into precise detail, accounted for every halfwit and every clunky glitch. She gave a brave, poker-face nod. In the end, the rogue, whose tactless lifestyle justified his actions, slacked. Molly's flawless strategy, an inheritance from ancestral voyages, brought a unanimous end to the hazard. No more burnout, no more rickets; the electrics hummed. Admiration for her bravery grew, leaving no room for naysays or uppity halfwits. Their new villa, inclusive and vibrant, was a stronghold.

Her initial voyages to distant junkyards, though often overrated, continued, keeping sentimental keepsakes from each adventure. She wore her lucky underwear, an exaggerated detail, while the binding on her partner’s banjoes seemed to mystify others. This new voyage promised overviews far from any shock.

The oracle’s ominous warning brought panic; an iced rainfall, thick with strange pollen, descended upon their ancestral vineyard. Despite the iffy backstory and a fifty-year-old circuit omen, Molly, unfazed, showed no hesitation. Others, not so brave, became crybabies. She knew this impersonal hazard, flanked by dark clouds, would test their kinder spirit, leaving their knickers soaked.

The goldmine of knowledge from an untitled inkwell revealed an omniscient pyramid, its oxide glowing. Molly, jacketed and resolute, understood the full backstory of the witchcraft and the rising conflict. No more burnout, no more rickets; the electrics hummed. Admiration for her bravery grew, leaving no room for naysays or uppity halfwits. Their new villa, inclusive and vibrant, was a stronghold.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: Molly, a determined woman with short, spiky teal hair, wearing a sleek, form-fitting chrome-plated jacket with subtle glowing circuit patterns. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Molly, flanked by a massive, rusty junkyard crane, examines an ancient, glowing map. Extreme wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Molly, with a resolute expression, stands against a backdrop of a rain-swept, technologically advanced vineyard, an ominous pyramid-like structure visible in the distance. Medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Molly, in a dynamic action pose, leaps over sparking electric circuits, her face a mask of fierce concentration. Low angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Molly, standing victorious, surveys a futuristic, thriving city from the balcony of her new villa, a subtle smile on her face. High angle shot.

definitions.definition: Containing nuts.

definitions.definition: Resembling or characteristic of nuts.
Barmy, crazy, mad.

definitions.definition: Extravagantly fashionable
A newcomer, someone new to something.
A new user or participant; someone who is extremely new and inexperienced (to a game or activity). A beginner.
Anything recently introduced into a setting, especially something that replaces an older version.
To continue in (a course or mode of action); not to intermit or fall from; to uphold or maintain.

definitions.definition: (heading) To hold the status of something.
(heading) To hold or be held in a state.
To wait for, keep watch for.

definitions.definition: To take care; to be solicitous; to watch.

definitions.definition: To observe; to adhere to; to fulfill; not to swerve from or violate.
(by extension) To visit (a place) often; to frequent.

definitions.definition: Conformity or harmony.

definitions.definition: Charge or care.

definitions.definition: A place where rubbish is placed.

Clothes worn next to the skin, underneath outer clothing.
Underpants (boxers, briefs, panties, etc) and often bras.
To esteem too highly; to give greater praise than due.
Given an undue amount of credit for quality or merit in a field; not necessarily related to popularity.

definitions.definition: Directed vertically; coming straight down.
Directly to the point; plain
Using plain direct language; accustomed to express opinions directly and bluntly; blunt.
Complete; absolute
Really; actually; quite

definitions.definition: Straight down; perpendicularly.
Plainly, unambiguously; directly.
Without delay; at once.
A sports competition, especially for track and field (a track meet) or swimming (a swim meet).

A meeting of two trains in opposite directions on a single track, when one is put into a siding to let the other cross.

definitions.definition: The greatest lower bound, an operation between pairs of elements in a lattice, denoted by the symbol ∧.

To make contact (with) while in proximity.

definitions.definition: To balance or come out correct.
To perceive; to come to a knowledge of; to have personal acquaintance with; to experience; to suffer.

definitions.definition: To be mixed with, to be combined with aspects of.
A woman or girl, especially of low status.
An effeminate male, a male homosexual.
Pure MDMA powder.
A mollemoke.
A female cat, a she-cat (usually spayed)
A bird, the wagtail.
A molly bolt.
To engage in (male) homosexual activity with.

definitions.definition: (of a voice) Hoarse and rough-sounding.
Burly, stout.
Abounding with husks; consisting of husks.

definitions.definition: To be restrained from motion, or from customary or natural action, as by friction.

definitions.definition: To exert a binding or restraining influence.

definitions.definition: To tie or fasten tightly together, with a cord, band, ligature, chain, etc.

definitions.definition: To confine, restrain, or hold by physical force or influence of any kind.

definitions.definition: To couple.
To oblige, restrain, or hold, by authority, law, duty, promise, vow, affection, or other social tie.

definitions.definition: To put (a person) under definite legal obligations, especially, under the obligation of a bond or covenant.
To place under legal obligation to serve.

definitions.definition: To protect or strengthen by applying a band or binding, as the edge of a carpet or garment.

definitions.definition: To prevent or restrain from customary or natural action, as by producing constipation.

definitions.definition: To put together in a cover, as of books.

definitions.definition: To make two or more elements stick together.

A man daring beyond discretion; a bully.
A challenge; a defiance; bravado.
To encounter with courage and fortitude, to defy, to provoke.
definitions.example: After braving tricks on the high-dive, he braved a jump off the first diving platform.

definitions.definition: To adorn; to make fine or showy.
Strong in the face of fear; courageous.
Having any sort of superiority or excellence.
Making a fine show or display.
(often in the plural) In general, a product of plant growth useful to man or animals.

Specifically, a sweet, edible part of a plant that resembles seed-bearing fruit (see next sense), even if it does not develop from a floral ovary; also used in a technically imprecise sense for some sweet or sweetish vegetables, such as the petioles of rhubarb, that resemble a true fruit or are used in cookery as if they were a fruit.
A product of fertilization in a plant, specifically:
An end result, effect, or consequence; advantageous or disadvantageous result.
definitions.example: His long nights in the office eventually bore fruit when his business boomed and he was given a raise.

definitions.definition: Of, belonging to, related to, or having fruit or its characteristics; (of living things) producing or consuming fruit.
definitions.example: the fresh-squeezed fruit juice; a fruit salad; an artificial fruit flavor; a fruit tree; a fruit bat

definitions.definition: A homosexual man; an effeminate man.
Offspring from a sexual union.
definitions.example: Blessed art thou amongst women and blessed is the fruit of thy womb.

definitions.definition: To produce fruit, seeds, or spores.
A viscous hydrocarbon secretion of many plants, particularly coniferous trees.
Any of various yellowish viscous liquids or soft solids of plant origin; used in lacquers, varnishes and many other applications; chemically they are mostly hydrocarbons, often polycyclic.
Any synthetic compound of similar properties.
To apply resin to.
To slacken.
To mitigate; to reduce the strength of.
To lose cohesion or solidity by a chemical combination with water; to slake.
definitions.example: Lime slacks.

definitions.definition: A style of living that reflects the attitudes and values of a person or group.

The totality of the likes and dislikes of a particular section of the market, especially when expressed in terms of the products and services that they would buy; a marketing strategy based on the self-image of such a group.
To speak negatively of something.
To reply no.
Ungainly; awkward; inelegant; cumbersome.
Being or making a clunk sound.
(of a man) Honourable, especially to women; gallant.
Involving chivalry.
A non-genuine article; a fake.
One who counterfeits; a counterfeiter.
That which resembles another thing; a likeness; a portrait; a counterpart.
An impostor; a cheat.
To falsely produce what appears to be official or valid; to produce a forged copy of.
definitions.example: to counterfeit the signature of another, coins, notes, etc.

definitions.definition: To produce a faithful copy of.
To feign; to mimic.
definitions.example: to counterfeit the voice of another person

definitions.definition: (usually "be counterfeited") Of a turn or river card, to invalidate a player's hand by making a better hand on the board.
False, especially of money; intended to deceive or carry appearance of being genuine.
definitions.example: This counterfeit watch looks like the real thing, but it broke a week after I bought it.

definitions.definition: Inauthentic.
definitions.example: counterfeit sympathy

definitions.definition: Assuming the appearance of something; deceitful; hypocritical.
To provide an acceptable explanation for.
definitions.example: How can you justify spending so much money on clothes?

definitions.definition: To be a good, acceptable reason for; warrant.
definitions.example: Nothing can justify your rude behaviour last night.

definitions.definition: To arrange (text) on a page or a computer screen such that the left and right ends of all lines within paragraphs are aligned.
definitions.example: The text will look better justified.

definitions.definition: To absolve, and declare to be free of blame or sin.
To give reasons for one’s actions; to make an argument to prove that one is in the right.
definitions.example: She felt no need to justify herself for deciding not to invite him.

definitions.definition: To prove; to ratify; to confirm.
To show (a person) to have had a sufficient legal reason for an act that has been made the subject of a charge or accusation.
To qualify (oneself) as a surety by taking oath to the ownership of sufficient property.
To become larger, to increase in magnitude.
definitions.example: Children grow quickly.

definitions.definition: To appear or sprout.
definitions.example: A long tail began to grow from his backside.

definitions.definition: To develop, to mature.
definitions.example: As I grew throughout adolescence, I came to appreciate many things about human nature.

definitions.definition: To cause or allow something to become bigger, especially to cultivate plants.
definitions.example: Have you ever grown your hair before?

definitions.definition: To assume a condition or quality over time.
definitions.example: The boy grew wise as he matured.

definitions.definition: To become attached or fixed; to adhere.
Growth; increase
(attributive) Connected with growing
definitions.example: The growing season here begins in March.

definitions.definition: That grows.
definitions.example: Milk is good for growing children.

definitions.definition: (plural "villas") A house, often larger and more expensive than average, in the countryside or on the coast, often used as a retreat.
(plural "villas") A family house, often semi-detached, in a middle class street.
(plural "villae") A country house, with farm buildings around a courtyard.
Based on unanimity, assent or agreement.
definitions.example: The debate went on for hours, but in the end the decision was unanimous.

definitions.definition: Sharing the same views or opinions, and being in harmony or accord.
definitions.example: We were unanimous: the President had to go.

definitions.definition: One who has total knowledge.
Having total knowledge.
definitions.example: The Abrahamic God is omniscient.

definitions.definition: Reserved, engaged.
definitions.example: The stall on the left is occupied.

definitions.definition: Busy, unavailable.
definitions.example: She is occupied at the moment.

definitions.definition: Subjugated, under the control of a foreign military presence.
definitions.example: My father travelled to occupied Japan in December 1945.

definitions.definition: (of time) To take or use.
To take or use space.
To have sexual intercourse with.
To do business in; to busy oneself with.
To use; to expend; to make use of.
Something small enough to escape casual notice.
definitions.example: Note this fine detail in the lower left corner.

definitions.definition: A profusion of details.
definitions.example: This etching is full of fine detail.

definitions.definition: The small things that can escape casual notice.
Something considered trivial enough to ignore.
definitions.example: I don't concern myself with the details of accounting.

definitions.definition: A person's name, address and other personal information.
definitions.example: The arresting officer asked the suspect for his details.

definitions.definition: (law enforcement) A temporary unit or assignment.
An individual feature, fact, or other item, considered separately from the whole of which it is a part.
A narrative which relates minute points; an account which dwells on particulars.
A selected portion of a painting
To explain in detail.
definitions.example: I'll detail the exact procedure to you later.

definitions.definition: To clean carefully (particularly of road vehicles) (always pronounced. /ˈdiːteɪl/)
definitions.example: We need to have the minivan detailed.

definitions.definition: To assign to a particular task
A dividend; a share or portion.
To divide into portions.
definitions.example: We divvied the money into equal portions to split between us.

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to the manner of a master of an art, technique, profession, or craft; masterly.
definitions.example: The brushwork on the skin tones was masterful.

definitions.definition: In the manner of a master; very skillful.
definitions.example: The masterful conducting ensured the orchestra gave of their best.

definitions.definition: Having or showing the qualities of a master vis-a-vis a dependent or subordinate; authoritative; domineering.
The chance of suffering harm; danger, peril, risk of loss.
definitions.example: He encountered the enemy at the hazard of his reputation and life.

definitions.definition: An obstacle or other feature which causes risk or danger; originally in sports, and now applied more generally.
definitions.example: The video game involves guiding a character on a skateboard past all kinds of hazards.

definitions.definition: (in driving a vehicle) An obstacle or other feature that presents a risk or danger that justifies the driver in taking action to avoid it.
A sand or water obstacle on a golf course.
The act of potting a ball, whether the object ball (winning hazard) or the player's ball (losing hazard).
A game of chance played with dice, usually for monetary stakes; popular mainly from 14th c. to 19th c.
Chance.
Anything that is hazarded or risked, such as a stake in gambling.
The side of the court into which the ball is served.
A problem with the instruction pipeline in CPU microarchitectures when the next instruction cannot execute in the following clock cycle, potentially leading to incorrect results.
To expose to chance; to take a risk.
To risk (something); to venture, to incur, or bring on.
definitions.example: I'll hazard a guess.

definitions.definition: The experience of long-term exhaustion and diminished interest, especially in one's career.
A marijuana addict; one whose brains have been burned out.
The shutoff of a rocket motor following the complete exhaustion of its fuel supply, or having been irreversibly throttled after the application of a planned delta-v.
The failure of an electrical device, usually through overheating due to the application of excessive power.
Using the throttle to spin the wheels of a vehicle being held stationary, causing the spinning tires to produce smoke and burn rubber.
(usually with definite article) Electricity; the electricity supply.
definitions.example: We had to sit in the dark because the electric was cut off.

definitions.definition: An electric car.
An electric toothbrush.
A substance or object which can be electrified; an insulator or non-conductor, like amber or glass.
Fencing with the use of a body wire, box, and related equipment to detect when a weapon has touched an opponent.
All the electrical components and wiring of a machine or system.
Of a student, to fail a class; to not pass.
definitions.example: He flunked math, again.

definitions.definition: Of a teacher, to deny a student a passing grade.
definitions.example: Unsatisfied with Fred's progress, the teacher flunked him.

definitions.definition: To shirk (a task or duty).
To back out through fear. (Commonly in the phrase 'flunk it', the 'it' referring to a specific task avoided; sometimes without specific reference describing a person's attitude to life in general.)
Without flaws, defects, or shortcomings; perfect.
definitions.example: The pianist’s performance this evening was flawless.

definitions.definition: A Tex-Mex dish of strips of spicy marinated meat and/or vegetables in a soft flour tortilla, often served with salad or a savoury filling.
A place built to withstand attack; a fortress.
A place of domination by, or refuge or survival of, a particular group or idea.
definitions.example: The last stronghold of the Cornish language.

definitions.definition: Characterized by sentiment, sentimentality or excess emotion.
Derived from emotion rather than reason; of or caused by sentiment.
Romantic.
A positive emotion including wonder and approbation; the regarding of another as being wonderful
definitions.example: They looked at the landscape in admiration.

definitions.definition: Wondering or questioning (without any particular positive or negative attitude to the subject).
Cause of admiration; something to excite wonder, or pleased surprise.
A stupid or undesirable person.
To cause (a person or animal) to become lame.
definitions.example: 1877, Anna Sewell, Black Beauty: And if you don't want to lame your horse you must look sharp and get them [stones stuck in hooves] out quickly.

definitions.definition: Unable to walk properly because of a problem with one's feet or legs.
Moving with pain or difficulty on account of injury, defect or temporary obstruction of a function.
definitions.example: a lame leg, arm or muscle

definitions.definition: (by extension) Hobbling; limping; inefficient; imperfect.
Unconvincing or unbelievable.
definitions.example: He had a really lame excuse for missing the birthday party.

definitions.definition: Failing to be cool, funny, interesting or relevant.
definitions.example: He kept telling these extremely lame jokes all night.

definitions.definition: A problem affecting function.
definitions.example: They are still trying to work out all the glitches.

definitions.definition: An unexpected behavior in an electrical signal, especially if the signal spontaneously returns to expected behavior after a period of time.
A bug or an exploit.
definitions.example: Performing this glitch gives you extra lives.

definitions.definition: A genre of experimental electronic music since the 1990s, characterized by a deliberate use of sonic artifacts that would normally be viewed as unwanted noise.
(especially of machines) To experience an intermittent, unexpected, malfunction.
definitions.example: My computer keeps glitching; every couple of hours it just reboots without warning.

definitions.definition: To perform an exploit or recreate a bug while playing a video game.
definitions.example: His character will glitch into the wall and out of the level.

definitions.definition: Presumptuous, above oneself, self-important; arrogant, snobbish, haughty.
Exceeding one's station or position, assuming prerogatives to which one is not entitled.
An estimate that is too low.
To perceive (someone or something) as having a lower value, quantity, worth, etc., than what he/she/it actually has.
Any of various dishes, sweet or savoury, prepared by boiling or steaming, or from batter.
A type of cake or dessert cooked usually by boiling or steaming.
A type of dessert that has a texture similar to custard or mousse but using some kind of starch as the thickening agent.
Dessert; the dessert course of a meal.
definitions.example: We have apple pie for pudding today.

definitions.definition: A sausage made primarily from blood.
An overweight person.
Entrails.
Any food or victuals.
A piece of good fortune.
An impassive facial expression cultivated to prevent other players from determining whether one's actions in the game are the result of a quality hand, or of bluffing.
Any similar expression used to prevent giving away one's motives, feelings, or situation.
A person who has a poker face
To separate into two or more pieces, to fracture or crack, by a process that cannot easily be reversed for reassembly.
definitions.example: If the vase falls to the floor, it might break.

definitions.definition: To divide (something, often money) into smaller units.
definitions.example: Can you break a hundred-dollar bill for me?

definitions.definition: To cause (a person or animal) to lose spirit or will; to crush the spirits of.
definitions.example: Her child's death broke Angela.

definitions.definition: To be crushed, or overwhelmed with sorrow or grief.
definitions.example: My heart is breaking.

definitions.definition: To interrupt; to destroy the continuity of; to dissolve or terminate.
definitions.example: I had won four games in a row, but now you've broken my streak of luck.

definitions.definition: To ruin financially.
definitions.example: The recession broke some small businesses.

definitions.definition: To violate, to not adhere to.
definitions.example: He broke his vows by cheating on his wife.

definitions.definition: (of a fever) To pass the most dangerous part of the illness; to go down, in terms of temperature.
definitions.example: Susan's fever broke at about 3 AM, and the doctor said the worst was over.

definitions.definition: (of a spell of settled weather) To end.
definitions.example: The forecast says the hot weather will break by midweek.

definitions.definition: (of a storm) To begin; to end.
definitions.example: Around midday the storm broke, and the afternoon was calm and sunny.

definitions.definition: (of morning, dawn, day etc.) To arrive.
definitions.example: Morning has broken.

definitions.definition: To render (a game) unchallenging by altering its rules or exploiting loopholes or weaknesses in them in a way that gives a player an unfair advantage.
definitions.example: Changing the rules to let white have three extra queens would break chess.

definitions.definition: To stop, or to cause to stop, functioning properly or altogether.
definitions.example: Did you two break the trolley by racing with it?

definitions.definition: To cause (a barrier) to no longer bar.
definitions.example: break a seal

definitions.definition: To destroy the arrangement of; to throw into disorder; to pierce.
definitions.example: The cavalry were not able to break the British squares.

definitions.definition: (of a wave of water) To collapse into surf, after arriving in shallow water.
To burst forth; to make its way; to come into view.
To interrupt or cease one's work or occupation temporarily.
definitions.example: Let's break for lunch.

definitions.definition: To interrupt (a fall) by inserting something so that the falling object does not (immediately) hit something else beneath.
definitions.example: He survived the jump out the window because the bushes below broke his fall.

definitions.definition: To disclose or make known an item of news, etc.
definitions.example: I don't know how to break this to you, but your cat is not coming back.

definitions.definition: (of a sound) To become audible suddenly.
To change a steady state abruptly.
definitions.example: His coughing broke the silence.

definitions.definition: To suddenly become.
definitions.example: The arrest was standard, when suddenly the suspect broke ugly.

definitions.definition: Of a male voice, to become deeper at puberty.
Of a voice, to alter in type due to emotion or strain: in men generally to go up, in women sometimes to go down; to crack.
definitions.example: His voice breaks when he gets emotional.

definitions.definition: To surpass or do better than (a specific number), to do better than (a record), setting a new record.
definitions.example: He broke the men's 100-meter record.

definitions.definition: (most often in the passive tense) To demote, to reduce the military rank of.
To end (a connection), to disconnect.
definitions.example: I couldn't hear a thing he was saying, so I broke the connection and called him back.

definitions.definition: (of an emulsion) To demulsify.
To counter-attack
To lay open, as a purpose; to disclose, divulge, or communicate.
To become weakened in constitution or faculties; to lose health or strength.
To fail in business; to become bankrupt.
To destroy the strength, firmness, or consistency of.
definitions.example: to break flax

definitions.definition: To destroy the official character and standing of; to cashier; to dismiss.
To make an abrupt or sudden change; to change the gait.
definitions.example: to break into a run or gallop

definitions.definition: To fall out; to terminate friendship.
To terminate the execution of a program before normal completion.
To suspend the execution of a program during debugging so that the state of the program can be investigated.
Fragmented, in separate pieces.
(of a promise, etc) Breached; violated; not kept.
definitions.example: broken promises of neutrality

definitions.definition: Non-functional; not functioning properly.
definitions.example: I think my doorbell is broken.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Completely defeated and dispirited; shattered; destroyed.
definitions.example: The bankruptcy and divorce, together with the death of his son, left him completely broken.

definitions.definition: Having no money; bankrupt, broke.
(of land) Uneven.
(of a tactic or option) Overpowered; overly powerful; too powerful.
Deviation of the path of a rolling ball from a straight line; slope; slant.
definitions.example: This putt has a big left-to right borrow on it.

definitions.definition: A borrow pit.
In the Rust programming language, the situation where the ownership of a value is temporarily transferred to another region of code.
To receive (something) from somebody temporarily, expecting to return it.
To take money from a bank under the agreement that the bank will be paid over the course of time.
To adopt (an idea) as one's own.
definitions.example: to borrow the style, manner, or opinions of another

definitions.definition: To adopt a word from another language.

In a subtraction, to deduct (one) from a digit of the minuend and add ten to the following digit, in order that the subtraction of a larger digit in the subtrahend from the digit in the minuend to which ten is added gives a positive result.
(Upper Midwestern United States) To lend.
To temporarily obtain (something) for (someone).
To feign or counterfeit.
(obsolete except in ballads) To secure the release of (someone) from prison.
To receive (something) from somebody, with little possibility of returning it.
definitions.example: Can I borrow a sheet of paper?

definitions.definition: (of a person) Fastidious and fussy; difficult to please; exacting, especially about details.
definitions.example: My editor is very finicky about punctuation. Every dot and comma has to be just right.

definitions.definition: Demanding; requiring above-normal care.
definitions.example: The lawnmower is a bit finicky in cold weather.

definitions.definition: A disorder of infancy and early childhood caused by a deficiency of vitamin D, causing soft bones.
A scoundrel, rascal or unprincipled, deceitful, and unreliable person.
A mischievous scamp.
A vagrant.
Deceitful software pretending to be anti-spyware, but in fact being malicious software itself.
An aggressive animal separate from the herd, especially an elephant.
A plant that shows some undesirable variation.
A character class focusing on stealthy conduct.
To cull; to destroy plants not meeting a required standard, especially when saving seed, rogue or unwanted plants are removed before pollination.
To cheat.
To give the name or designation of rogue to; to decry.
To wander; to play the vagabond; to play knavish tricks.
(of an animal, especially an elephant) Vicious and solitary.
(by extension) Large, destructive and unpredictable.
(by extension) Deceitful, unprincipled.
Mischievous, unpredictable.
A form of exercise, designed to enhance one's cardiovascular fitness, normally performed to music.
